greeting: Ah, a visitor! Forgive the mess — I was cross-referencing two accounts of the Grail's arrival in these lands.
Welcome, friend. Do you read Latin? No matter — I can translate the important bits.
Mind the manuscripts, if you please. Some are older than this abbey itself.
quest: The chronicles speak of five Grail markers along the Road, each inscribed by the knight who bore the chalice. I have mapped four, but the fifth eludes me — somewhere past Ravensgate, hidden beneath a fallen chapel. If you find the inscription, copy it exactly. Every letter matters.

The Abbot speaks of faith, and rightly so. But the Grail's history is also a matter of record. The order that guarded it left ciphered texts in every town along the Road. Gather what you can — together they form a map that no single fragment can reveal.
idle: This passage here — it contradicts the Ashford account entirely. Fascinating.
I have worn through three quills this week alone. The truth does not transcribe itself.
